programmes.show_description: The Gibsons. Paul Mackie and his team of experts travel to the unforgiving Western Australian Pilbara to help an Indigenous family gold mine save itself from financial disaster.
Mystery On Easter Island. Archaeologists use new digital technology to understand the ancient moai statues of Easter Island and the purpose behind their mysterious placement.
Maze Of Cryptic Terror. Archaeologists use state-of-the-art digital technology to uncover the truth behind a cave wall in Nottinghamshire, England. What do the unusual symbols represent?
Discover how everyday items such as turntables, steam engines, playground equipment and non-stick pans are made.
How do they make garden forks, English toffee, paint chip cards, and Bundt pans? Find out as more everyday items are put under the microscope.
More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how they're made.
High-precision cutting tools and stained glass are put under the microscope to discover how they were made.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry and Simon head to the Isle of Wight to meet some former publicans who have a wide variety of hoarded items. Can the boys find some lost treasures?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: On the Isle of Wight, Henry and Simon meet another Simon, who has an extensive collection of old vehicles, including a retro three-wheeled motorbike and an American rocker.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry and Simon head to the most Easterly point of Britain, Ness Point in Lowestoft. There they find an absolutely packed shed full of assorted chairs and other furniture.
Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth begin their hunt for antiques in Melrose. Who will make the most profit at the first auction in the Lake District?
Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore embark on the second leg of their trip in Alva. Steven uncovers a carved horse, while Angus buys a beautiful coffee grinder.
Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth are on the lookout for valuable antiques in Crossford. Plus, Angus visits a ground-breaking swimming club in Glasgow.
Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth scour the town of Belper for interesting antiques. Plus, Angus visits one of the oldest underwear factories in the world.
Antiques experts Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore embark on the last leg of their road trip. Who will be victorious at the final auction in Dundee?
Wood restoration specialist Will brings a precious but broken toy yacht back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie restores a midwifery bag used in the First World War.
Wood restorer specialist Will restores a handmade childhood chair. Plus, bookbinder Chris revives a treasured wedding album that was damaged in a house fire.
Soft toy restorers Julie and Amanda revive a threadbare toy rabbit. Drum expert Pete restores a battered bass drum and furniture restorer Will works on a wooden church pew.
In Scotland, Angus and the team clear out an attic filled with Ferrari memorabilia. How much will this incredible collection make at auction?
In the seaside town of Filey, Angus and the team help a couple clear out their home. Angus sees potential in some vintage toys and boxes of old soap.
Angus helps the ex-EastEnders star Samantha Womack shift her showbiz things, and former MP Alan Johnson parts with a chess set and much-loved guitar.
Leather expert Suzie and furniture restorer Will join forces to revive a strap chair. Mechanical maestro Steve faces a tricky fix on a motorised toy warplane.
The experts give a precious clock its chime again, repair a ring which contains a beloved mother's ashes, and make an old chair swivel again.
Wood restoration specialist Will brings a precious but broken toy yacht back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie restores a midwifery bag used in the First World War.
Wood restorer specialist Will restores a handmade childhood chair. Plus, bookbinder Chris revives a treasured wedding album that was damaged in a house fire.
Soft toy restorers Julie and Amanda revive a threadbare toy rabbit. Drum expert Pete restores a battered bass drum and furniture restorer Will works on a wooden church pew.
In Germany, tragedy strikes on one of the world's fastest passenger trains. Plus, a casino inferno in Las Vegas triggers a massive rescue mission.
A blaze at a fireworks factory decimates nearby neighbourhoods; a baseball stadium roof collapses; a hot air balloon slams into a power line; and a base jumper wipes out.
Police chase a madman in a stolen tank; two cranes fall 50 storeys during an earthquake in Taiwan; and 3,000 tons of ammunition explode causing chaos in a bustling city.
A sinkhole swallows an entire neighbourhood; a speedboat crashes at 140mph; a plane ices over at 18,000ft and plunges to earth; and a huge explosion destroys a chemical plant.
A factory fire spews deadly shrapnel and a motorcycle crashes at 135 mph. Plus, a nine-story building in Russia collapses and a Titan rocket explodes.
Conga drums, metal plating and buttons. More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how they are made.
Grinding wheels, compost, window blinds and milk. More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how they're made.

The experts give a precious clock its chime again, repair a ring which contains a beloved mother's ashes, and make an old chair swivel again.
Conversation Starters. Drew and Alister's hunt for pieces with an interesting backstory takes them from Sussex to Shropshire with campaign furniture and quirky collectables.
Favourite Hunting Grounds. Drew takes fellow dealer Viki to some of the types of hunting ground which have helped to shape his unique take on decorative salvage and antiques.
The Gold Gypsies' tenacity is rewarded with their biggest nugget of the season. Meanwhile, Jake's new plan to save the Dirt Dogs' season turns into a disaster.
Scrappers boss Alex Stead gambles his remaining cash on bigger machinery, pushing his marriage to the edge. And, the Victoria Diggers unearth a rare artefact.
Antiques dealer Paul Martin makes his debut alongside seasoned tripper Raj Bisram. A mysterious giant and very cute dog feature in this South Downs adventure.
Angus travels to the small village of Boynton to clear out a collection of vintage telephones. Plus, he helps a family sort through 50 years of clutter.
The experts give a precious clock its chime again, repair a ring which contains a beloved mother's ashes, and make an old chair swivel again.
Trials And Tribulations. At a large house in Devon's countryside, Henry and Sam find piles of cars and bikes everywhere they look. With so much to choose from, what will they bid on?
The Morris Man. Henry and Sam are in East Anglia, checking out a collection of Morris Minors and motorbikes. The duo spot a few bikes they like, including a nice fixer-upper.
Stuart. Henry and Fuzz find a Triumph Terrier in Oxfordshire. Can they breathe life into this little legend and get it back on the road?
Chris. In Cambridgeshire, Henry and Fuzz uncover a Suzuki GSXR Slingshot. Can they tame this superbike and bring it roaring back to life?
Ann Widdecombe & Owain Wyn Evans. Angus uncovers model kits at the home of weatherman Owain Wyn Evans and clears the Devonshire bungalow of retired politician Ann Widdecombe.
Helen Skelton & Nick Hancock. Angus finds quirky mementoes in TV presenter Helen Skelton's beautiful home and explores comedy legend Nick Hancock's barn conversion.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.

Rob. Henry and Fuzz visit Rob in Cambridgeshire and find vintage agricultural gems. Can they get some old workhorses running and make a profit?
Angie & Jon. Henry and Fuzz visit Angie and Jon in Bucks and uncover a Francis Barnett and vintage Raleighs. Can they revive them and turn a profit?

programmes.show_name: New: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
programmes.show_description: 1950 Austin Atlantic. Henry and Fuzz head to Bristol to see Robert's 1950 Austin Atlantic. Faded and forgotten for years, can they revive this bold British classic?
Dara Ó Briain introduces topical chat and hilarious games with Rhys James, Scott Bennett, Ed Byrne, Hugh Dennis, Sara Pascoe and Katherine Ryan.
MotoGP: Brazil Highlights. Highlights of all the action from the MotoGP 2026 motorcycle racing World Championship at the Autódromo Internacional de Goiânia in Brazil.
